title: lysimachia paridiformis
intro: Lysimachia paridiformis, also known as the sweet woodruff, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Primulaceae. It is native to Europe and Asia, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antispasmodic and carminative. The plant contains coumarin, which has been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an anti-anxiety agent. Sweet woodruff is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or dried herb, and is used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.
